message:
test(core): time-travel / commit-seq oracle conformance tests

Add three new conformance_oracle_ext tests exercising temporal
query support end-to-end against rusqlite as the oracle, plus a
small framework expansion to make it easier to compare
fsqlite SELECT results against rusqlite on identical DDL/DML
sequences.

crates/fsqlite-core/tests/conformance_oracle_ext.rs

- Extend CONFORMANCE_COVERAGE_BUCKETS with a new
  `temporal_and_time_travel` bucket (patterns: time_travel,
  system_time, commitseq, temporal) with minimum_tests=3, so the
  conformance coverage audit keeps us honest about landing
  temporal tests as the feature grows.

- New formatting helpers:
    format_fsqlite_value(&SqliteValue) -> String
    format_rusqlite_value(rusqlite::types::Value) -> String
  Both produce the same canonical textual form (NULL, integer,
  float, 'text', X'hex') so row-by-row comparisons can surface
  human-readable diffs on failure.

- New query harness helpers, all reusable across future oracle
  tests:
    query_fsqlite_strings(conn, sql) -> Vec<Vec<String>>
    query_rusqlite_strings(conn, sql) -> Vec<Vec<String>>
    compare_query_pair(f_conn, r_conn, sql) — returns a Result
        carrying (fsqlite_rows, rusqlite_rows) when they disagree.
    assert_query_pair_matches(f_conn, r_conn, sql) — panics with
        a side-by-side formatted diff when compare_query_pair
        detects divergence.
    apply_fsqlite_statements / apply_rusqlite_statements — run a
        slice of &str statements and panic-with-context on first
        failure.
    build_rusqlite_snapshot(statements) — construct a fresh
        in-memory rusqlite Connection pre-populated with the
        supplied DDL/DML.

- Three new #[test] cases under the new bucket:

    test_conformance_time_travel_commitseq_historical_state_s76j
      — builds a table, issues a sequence of UPDATEs, captures
        commit sequence numbers at each step, and asserts that
        `SELECT ... AS OF COMMITSEQ N` returns the historical
        state that matches rusqlite's view after rewinding its
        own snapshot chain.

    test_conformance_time_travel_delete_update_visibility_s76k
      — verifies that DELETEd rows are visible in historical
        AS OF queries at a pre-DELETE commitseq and invisible
        after, and that UPDATEd rows show the pre-UPDATE value
        when queried at the pre-UPDATE commitseq.  This pins the
        delete/update visibility semantics of the temporal oracle
        against the "what SQLite would have returned at that
        point in time" baseline.

    test_conformance_time_travel_timestamp_and_live_state_preservation_s76l
      — runs a mixed workload of INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE interleaved
        with timestamp captures, then asserts that live (current)
        state queries continue to match rusqlite's live state even
        after the historical machinery has been exercised.  This
        is the regression guard against "temporal indexing quietly
        corrupts current state".

These tests document the bd-s76j/k/l temporal oracle bead IDs in
the test names so future bead audits can trace the implementation
back to the spec.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
